question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: FindByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted hash string to FindByHash() that matches a pre-genesis block, causing the block's consensus_start/consensus_end window to include fee transfers that should be excluded from the canonical ledger view, resulting in inflated or fabricated fee operations being returned to the caller?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findBlockByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a hash value that exploits the collate 'C' case-sensitive comparison in selectByHashWithIndex to match an unintended record_file row, shifting the consensus_end boundary and pulling additional fee-bearing transactions into the block's fee calculation window?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: FindByIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a large int64 index value to FindByIndex() that causes the selectRecordBlockByIndex subquery's @index + 1::bigint cast to overflow or wrap, producing an incorrect consensus_end boundary that expands the fee-collection window beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: selectRecordBlockByIndex] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the coalesce fallback in selectRecordBlockByIndex by querying the last known block index, causing consensus_end to equal the stored consensus_end rather than the next block's consensus_start - 1, thereby including extra transactions and their fee transfers in the block's fee set?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: selectByHashWithIndex] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coalesce in selectByHashWithIndex—where consensus_end falls back to the stored value when no next block exists—to obtain a block whose fee window extends to the current chain tip, causing all subsequent fee transfers to be attributed to that block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: initGenesisRecordFile()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition in initGenesisRecordFile() by sending concurrent requests before sync.Once fires, causing two goroutines to read different genesis recordBlock values from the database, with only one being stored, leading to an inconsistent genesis consensus_start that shifts fee attribution across blocks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: initGenesisRecordFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TOCTOU gap between the ConsensusStart != genesisConsensusStartUnset check and the sync.Once.Do() call in initGenesisRecordFile() to cause a stale or incorrect genesis block to be cached, permanently skewing the consensus_start boundary used for all subsequent fee window calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ToBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that resolves to a recordBlock whose Index equals genesisBlock.Index, causing ToBlock() to set parentHash = rb.Hash and parentIndex = rb.Index (self-referential), which when combined with fee queries produces a circular block reference that misattributes fee transfers to the genesis block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RetrieveLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user flood RetrieveLatest() with concurrent requests during a period when the latest block index equals the genesis block index, causing the rb.Index < br.genesisBlock.Index check to pass inconsistently and return a block with an incorrect fee window?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: FindByIdentifier()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a valid hash that belongs to block N but pass index M (M != N) to FindByIdentifier(), and by exploiting a race on genesisBlock initialization cause the index mismatch check to be evaluated against a stale genesis index, returning a block with a misaligned consensus window that distorts fee transfer attribution?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: selectGenesis] Can an unprivileged external user influence the account_balance table for the treasury_entity_id (e.g., by submitting transactions that create balance snapshots) to shift the genesis.timestamp used in selectGenesis, causing the genesis consensus_start to be set to genesis.timestamp + 1 instead of rf.consensus_start, thereby excluding legitimate fee transfers from the genesis block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: selectGenesis] Can an unprivileged external user cause the selectGenesis query to select a different record_file row by injecting transactions that push the earliest account_balance timestamp for treasury_entity_id forward, changing which record_file is considered genesis and thus altering the fee window for all blocks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findBlockByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a hash that resolves to a block with Index just below br.genesisBlock.Index, triggering the ErrBlockNotFound path in findBlockByHash() after the database query succeeds, and use the resulting error response timing to infer the genesis block index and then craft fee-window queries that straddle the genesis boundary?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RetrieveLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the selectLatestWithIndex query's ORDER BY index DESC LIMIT 1 behavior during a block reorganization or delayed ingestion to cause RetrieveLatest() to return a block with a consensus_end that does not reflect the true chain tip, causing fee transfers in the gap to be omitted from the returned block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: handleDatabaseError()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that consistently trigger gorm.ErrRecordNotFound in handleDatabaseError(), causing the caller to silently skip fee-bearing blocks and return an incomplete fee picture to downstream consumers relying on the Rosetta API?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: FindByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an empty string to FindByHash() to receive ErrInvalidArgument, and use the differential response (vs. a non-empty invalid hash) to enumerate valid hash prefixes via timing side-channels, ultimately identifying block hashes whose fee windows can be targeted for manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: selectByHashWithIndex] Can an unprivileged external user supply a hash string containing SQL metacharacters or Unicode sequences that survive the collate 'C' comparison and match an unintended record_file row, shifting the consensus_end boundary and causing fee transfers from a different block to be included in the returned block's fee set?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: initGenesisRecordFile()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests before the mirror node has ingested any account_balance records for treasury_entity_id, causing initGenesisRecordFile() to return ErrNodeIsStarting repeatedly, and then time a request to arrive exactly when the first balance record is written to cause a race that caches an incorrect genesis consensus_start, permanently distorting fee windows?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ToBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the genesis edge case in ToBlock() by querying a block whose Index equals genesisBlock.Index, causing consensusStart to be overwritten with genesisBlock.ConsensusStart, which if different from rb.ConsensusStart shifts the fee-collection window start and excludes or includes fee transfers that should not be in that block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: FindByIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user pass index = 0 to FindByIndex() when the genesis block has a non-zero index, causing findBlockByIndex() to return ErrBlockNotFound (since 0 < br.genesisBlock.Index), and use this to probe the genesis block index value and then craft targeted fee-window queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: selectRecordBlockByIndex] Can an unprivileged external user pass index = math.MaxInt64 - 1 to FindByIndex(), causing the @index + 1::bigint expression in selectRecordBlockByIndex to overflow to a negative value, making the subquery return no row and the coalesce fall back to consensus_end, thereby expanding the fee window to include all future transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: blockRepository] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that blockRepository.genesisBlock is a value type (not a pointer) and is written by sync.Once.Do() without a memory barrier visible to readers outside the once.Do closure, causing goroutines that read br.genesisBlock after the once fires to observe a partially initialized recordBlock with incorrect ConsensusStart, leading to wrong fee window boundaries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: initGenesisRecordFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic check-then-act pattern (ConsensusStart != genesisConsensusStartUnset checked outside sync.Once) in initGenesisRecordFile() to observe a window where genesisBlock.ConsensusStart is genesisConsensusStartUnset (-1) and use that sentinel value as a valid consensus_start, causing fee transfers timestamped near -1 nanoseconds to be incorrectly included in the genesis block?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RetrieveGenesis()] Can an unprivileged external user call RetrieveGenesis() concurrently with the first call to initGenesisRecordFile() to receive a block with ConsensusStart = genesisConsensusStartUnset (-1), causing downstream fee queries using this block's ConsensusStartNanos as a range boundary to include all transactions with timestamps >= -1, effectively capturing all fee transfers in the database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: selectGenesis] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high-volume of transactions to the treasury account to create many account_balance snapshots, causing the selectGenesis query's ORDER BY consensus_timestamp LIMIT 1 to select a different (later) genesis timestamp, shifting the genesis consensus_start and excluding early fee transfers from the genesis block's fee window?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/block.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: FindByHash()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted hash string to FindByHash() that matches a pre-genesis block, causing the block's consensus_start/consensus_end window to include fee transfers that should be excluded from the canonical ledger view, resulting in inflated or fabricated fee operations being returned to the caller?
